I mean, it's remarkable to me. On a paid basis, if that's what you're asking about, a very abbreviated answer to the question is this. When you rent them or buy them, hire them, there's different levels of usage and that has an impact on how much money. So there's everything from, I'm going to use your picture and this little statement, this innocuous non-endorsement statement, Paul McGuire is the most interesting guy I've ever met in my life and that's it. Then there's I'm going to use your picture and Paul McGuire knows more about real estate than anybody I've ever met in my life and that's going to cost you more. And then the more usage you want, typically the more money you're going to pay unless you are like fortune, unless you find the guy who desperately needs any money this week and they're there every week. Their needs change on a week to week, year to year basis. Celebrities are no different than any other population group. They continually sift and sort themselves into 5%, 15%, then 80%. So 80% of them are broke, 80% of them are about to have their Mercedes repossessed, 80% of them got all kinds of problems. And so there's always somebody who'll take small money. It's just a question of finding them. But usage sort of controls cost. And so you've got to think through all the usages you would want and then you've got to go shopping and you've got to figure out what your budget is and all of that. But there's ample empirical evidence that almost every usage more than pays for itself one way or another or multiple ways. Now when I say that, for example, let's take George Foreman speaking at Rory Fats Bootcamp this year. So George Foreman I think cost $75,000. And the usage Rory got was George's go be there to speak. He's going to get some pictures taken with him by his coaching group members. He recorded, I don't know, 10, 12 minutes worth of stuff that we used on tele-seminars. I took George Foreman's two books and rewrote a little booklet of success stuff by George Foreman. Also like one little mini chapter by George and then a chapter by Rory and then a chapter by George and a chapter by Rory that really was a pitch for the bootcamp. But looks like a little booklet from George. And that's about it. So now how can we get our $75,000 back and more? Well he could help attendance. That's one thing that could happen. And at, I don't know, $2,000 a piece. He doesn't have to get that many more there in order to justify his existence purely on that. He could help overall response by they open an envelope with George on it when they wouldn't have opened it otherwise they come to a tele-seminar that George is on where they wouldn't have come to a tele-seminar otherwise. And that now reflects in our registration numbers. But let's say he doesn't. Let's say our numbers year to year are the same. Well did we waste our $75,000? Not necessarily. There's also the mood of the people at the bootcamp at which we are selling coaching. So remember positive feelings, negative feelings. So if they have positive feelings because George Foreman's there and this is exciting and they can't wait to go home and tell everybody that they met George Foreman and they're excited about coming to meet George Foreman. And George is happy and funny and everybody likes George. And everybody in coaching gets to go get their picture taken with George and everybody who's not in coaching has to stand behind the line and watch people getting their picture taken with George. Now he could have a big impact on what happens on site even if he had no impact on what happens prior to on site. How else could he have impact? Well even though we bought the usage for this event and this event only, the history of this usage never goes away. So Rory with George at this event and we had George at this event, we had this person at this event, that person, that piles up and accumulates. And so now over a period of three or four years we have Rory with the with strategy with five or six or seven of these people not one and that makes Rory a celebrity because we have surrounded him with celebrities. How else does George pay his way?
